heading: Grants for research and development
label: 1255 Grants for research and development

children.heading: Demonstration projects covering storm waters, advanced waste treatment and water purification methods, and joint treatment systems for municipal and industrial wastes
children.label: a Demonstration projects covering storm waters, advanced waste treatment and water purification methods, and joint treatment systems for municipal and industrial wastes

children.text: any project which will demonstrate a new or improved method of preventing, reducing, and eliminating the discharge into any waters of pollutants from sewers which carry storm water or both storm water and pollutants; or

any project which will demonstrate advanced waste treatment and water purification methods (including the temporary use of new or improved chemical additives which provide substantial immediate improvements to existing treatment processes), or new or improved methods of joint treatment systems for municipal and industrial wastes;

children.heading: Demonstration projects for advanced treatment and environmental enhancement techniques to control pollution in river basins
children.label: b Demonstration projects for advanced treatment and environmental enhancement techniques to control pollution in river basins
children.text: The Administrator is authorized to make grants to any State or States or interstate agency to demonstrate, in river basins or portions thereof, advanced treatment and environmental enhancement techniques to control pollution from all sources, within such basins or portions thereof, including nonpoint sources, together with in stream water quality improvement techniques.1So in original.1

children.heading: Research and demonstration projects for prevention of water pollution by industry
children.label: c Research and demonstration projects for prevention of water pollution by industry
children.text: In order to carry out the purposes of , the Administrator is authorized to (1) conduct in the Environmental Protection Agency, (2) make grants to persons, and (3) enter into contracts with persons, for research and demonstration projects for prevention of pollution of any waters by industry including, but not limited to, the prevention, reduction, and elimination of the discharge of pollutants. No grant shall be made for any project under this subsection unless the Administrator determines that such project will develop or demonstrate a new or improved method of treating industrial wastes or otherwise prevent pollution by industry, which method shall have industrywide application.section 1311 of this title

children.heading: Accelerated and priority development of waste management and waste treatment methods and identification and measurement methods
children.label: d Accelerated and priority development of waste management and waste treatment methods and identification and measurement methods

children.text: waste management methods applicable to point and nonpoint sources of pollutants to eliminate the discharge of pollutants, including, but not limited to, elimination of runoff of pollutants and the effects of pollutants from inplace or accumulated sources;

advanced waste treatment methods applicable to point and nonpoint sources, including inplace or accumulated sources of pollutants, and methods for reclaiming and recycling water and confining pollutants so they will not migrate to cause water or other environmental pollution; and

improved methods and procedures to identify and measure the effects of pollutants on the chemical, physical, and biological integrity of water, including those pollutants created by new technological developments.

children.heading: Research and demonstration projects covering agricultural pollution and pollution from sewage in rural areas; dissemination of information
children.label: e Research and demonstration projects covering agricultural pollution and pollution from sewage in rural areas; dissemination of information

children.text: The Administrator is authorized to (A) make, in consultation with the Secretary of Agriculture, grants to persons for research and demonstration projects with respect to new and improved methods of preventing, reducing, and eliminating pollution from agriculture, and (B) disseminate, in cooperation with the Secretary of Agriculture, such information obtained under this subsection, , and as will encourage and enable the adoption of such methods in the agricultural industry.section 1254(p) of this titlesection 1314 of this title

The Administrator is authorized, (A) in consultation with other interested Federal agencies, to make grants for demonstration proj­ects with respect to new and improved methods of preventing, reducing, storing, collecting, treating, or otherwise eliminating pollution from sewage in rural and other areas where collection of sewage in conventional, community-wide sewage collection systems is impractical, uneconomical, or otherwise infeasible, or where soil conditions or other factors preclude the use of septic tank and drainage field systems, and (B) in cooperation with other interested Federal and State agencies, to disseminate such information obtained under this subsection as will encourage and enable the adoption of new and improved methods developed pursuant to this subsection.
No grant shall be made for any project unless such project shall have been approved by the appropriate State water pollution control agency or agencies and by the Administrator;
No grant shall be made for any project in an amount exceeding 75 per centum of cost thereof as determined by the Administrator; and
No grant shall be made for any project unless the Administrator determines that such project will serve as a useful demonstration for the purpose set forth in clause (1) or (2) of subsection (a).

children.heading: Maximum grants
children.label: g Maximum grants
children.text: Federal grants under subsections (c) and (d) of this section shall not exceed 75 per centum of the cost of the project.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: For the purpose of this section there is authorized to be appropriated $75,000,000 per fiscal year for the fiscal year ending , the fiscal year ending , and the fiscal year ending , and from such appropriations at least 10 per centum of the funds actually appropriated in each fiscal year shall be available only for the purposes of subsection (e).June 30, 1973June 30, 1974June 30, 1975

children.heading: Assistance for research and demonstration projects
children.label: i Assistance for research and demonstration projects
children.text: The Administrator is authorized to make grants to a municipality to assist in the costs of operating and maintaining a project which received a grant under this section, , or prior to , so as to reduce the operation and maintenance costs borne by the recipients of services from such project to costs comparable to those for projects assisted under subchapter II of this chapter.section 1254 of this titlesection 1263 of this titleDecember 27, 1977

children.heading: Assistance for recycle, reuse, and land treatment projects
children.label: j Assistance for recycle, reuse, and land treatment projects
children.text: The Administrator is authorized to make a grant to any grantee who received an increased grant pursuant to . Such grant may pay up to 100 per centum of the costs of technical evaluation of the operation of the treatment works, costs of training of persons (other than employees of the grantee), and costs of disseminating technical information on the operation of the treatment works.section 1282(a)(2) of this title
